concerns.text: Tasks T020-T024 explicitly forbid applying Arrhenius normalization and restrict analysis to 'Standard' (25°C, pH 7.4) records only. This violates spec FR-009 which mandates: 'System MUST normalize degradation rates to standard conditions... using the Arrhenius equation if assay conditions are available.' The tasks silently weaken the requirement to 'exclude' rather than 'normalize', violating the spec's scientific method.

Task T023b checks if '≥50% of the standard_subset' has pH/temp data to decide on covariate inclusion. Spec FR-004 requires checking '≥50% of records' (the full dataset). Restricting the check to the pre-filtered subset violates the spec's logic for covariate modeling, potentially excluding valid covariates present in the broader dataset.

Task T010's assertion for CalcWienerIndex contains placeholder text 'A minimal tolerance threshold will be applied...' instead of a concrete numerical value or reference. This violates the spec's requirement for testable acceptance criteria and the Constitution's 'Verified Accuracy' principle, as the test condition is undefined.

Task T025a conflates SC-002 (correlation significance) and SC-004 (residual diagnostics) into a single assertion block. The spec defines these as distinct success criteria with different thresholds (p < 0.05 for correlation vs p > 0.05 for normality). Merging them obscures traceability and violates the requirement to verify each SC independently.

Task T022a implements a sensitivity analysis sweeping thresholds over {0.4, 0.5, 0.6} as per spec Assumptions, but no corresponding Success Criterion (SC-*) exists to define how these results are measured or reported. This creates a task with no verifiable acceptance criteria against the spec, violating the 'no dropped acceptance criteria' rule.
